## Translating Data to Datawrapper

# 1. Overall Trend by Decade

# Line graph: one row per decade, median days as the value

```{r}
storms %>%
  #getting a look at how storms are named for cleaning purposes
  select(declarationTitle, incidentBeginDate, state, days_to_declaration) %>%
  head(20)
```

```{r dw-overall-trend}
dw_overall_trend <- storms %>%
  mutate(
    storm_name_clean = declarationTitle %>%
      str_extract("(HURRICANE|TROPICAL STORM) [A-Z]+") %>%
      #cleaned up the storm names to appear consistent & easy to read in datawrapper
      str_to_title(),
    year = year(incidentBeginDate)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    year >= 1998,
    # Filtered to 1998 onward per pre-NEMIS data limitation
    !is.na(storm_name_clean)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    hover_label = paste0(storm_name_clean, " (", state, ", ", year, ", ", days_to_declaration, " days)")
  ) %>%
  select(
    days_to_declaration,
    year,
    hover_label
  ) %>%
  arrange(year) %>%
  rename(
    `Days to Declaration` = days_to_declaration,
    `Year`                = year,
    `Storm`               = hover_label
  )

write.csv(dw_overall_trend, "dw_overall_trend.csv", row.names = FALSE)
dw_overall_trend
```

# 2. Helene by State

```{r dw-helene}
dw_helene <- helene_state_summary %>%
  select(state, min_lag, median_lag, max_lag) %>%
  arrange(median_lag) %>%
  rename(
    State           = state,
    `Min Days`      = min_lag,
    `Median Days`   = median_lag,
    `Max Days`      = max_lag
  )
#ultimately used median days, since all of these declarations had no variation between individual counties
write.csv(dw_helene, "dw_helene.csv", row.names = FALSE)
dw_helene
```

# 3. Tribal vs. Non-Tribal Declaration Lag

# Grouped bar graph: one row per storm-state event

# dw_tribal = all events; dw_tribal_fl = Florida only (which is what was ultimately used)

```{r dw-tribal}
df_raw <- read.csv("DR_state_tribal_level_same_state_Weather.csv")

dw_tribal <- df_raw %>%
  mutate(
    declarationDate     = as_date(declarationDate),
    incidentBeginDate   = as_date(incidentBeginDate),
    days_to_declaration = as.numeric(difftime(declarationDate, incidentBeginDate, units = "days")),
    request_type = case_when(
      tribalRequest == "Days - Tribal"     ~ "Tribal",
      tribalRequest == "Days - Not Tribal" ~ "Non-Tribal",
    ),
    event_label = paste0(
      str_to_title(incidentType), " - ",
      state, " (",
      format(incidentBeginDate, "%Y"), ")"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(event_label, days_to_declaration, request_type) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = request_type,
    values_from = days_to_declaration
  ) %>%
  arrange(event_label)

dw_tribal_fl <- dw_tribal %>%
  filter(str_detect(event_label, "- FL "))

write.csv(dw_tribal,    "dw_tribal.csv",    row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(dw_tribal_fl, "dw_tribal_fl.csv", row.names = FALSE)
#in datawrapper, I changed the event label to the storm name by referencing incident IDs associated with these tribal declarations from the Data Doc.

dw_tribal_fl
```
